rulings.Case Title: ESTATE OF WILLIAM SYKES VS. THOMASHA MAYFIELD et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, OCTOBER 9, 2007, LINE 1. DEFENDANT FREMONT INVESTMENT & LOAN'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained. Paragraph 32 states the cause of action is based on the continued claim of Fremont. Plaintiff has not alleged that Fremont knew the grant deed was false when published. The claim also appears to be based on Fremont's contentions in this litigation. Those claims are privileged and cannot be a basis for a slander of title claim in this case. Hearing to determine if there is a basis for plaintiff to amend. =(302/PJM/xMW)

rulings.Case Title: SOAP PROJECTS INC A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. EILEEN MARIE BUCKLEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Appeal Of The Order, Decision Award Of The Labor Commissioner
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, OCTOBER 9, 2007, LINE 11. RESPONDENT EILEEN BUCKLEY'S Motion To Dismiss Appeal Of The Order, Decision Award Of The Labor Commissioner is granted. The failure to appeal on time is a jurisdictional defect that cannot be cured. The judgment of $25,566.57, is to be paid from the bond. Plaintiff is entitled to reasonable attorney's fees of $2,100.00. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: GREG P DIXON VS. GEORGE VALVERDE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner Greg P. Dixon'S Order To Show Cause (Re Petition For Writ Of Mandate)
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, OCTOBER 9, 2007, LINE 13. Petitioner Greg P. Dixon's Order to Show Cause re: Petition for Writ of Mandate is denied. The record supports the ALJ's findings that petitioner did not complete the breath test. The record also supports the conclusion that the petitioner did not refuse to take the blood test. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: J.POWELL INVESTMENTS LLC et al VS. OTIS D TURNER et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, OCTOBER 9, 2007, LINE 15. DEFENDANTS OTIS TURNER and TERRANCE SMITH'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled, 5 days to answer. The complaint alleges in paragraphs 10 and 13 the expiration of the Notice to Quit. =(302/PJM)
